name: Transit Employees
description_short: Division 819 Transit Employees Credit Union is a financial cooperative serving transit employees in New Jersey with loans and credit card services.
description_long:
Division 819 Transit Employees Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative headquartered in Irvington, New Jersey. The credit union was established to serve the financial needs of transit employees, operating as a specialized financial institution focused on this membership base. Like traditional credit unions, it functions as a cooperative where members are both customers and part-owners of the institution.

The credit union offers a range of financial services including auto loans and credit card products. Members can access these services through their main website portal at www5.homecu.net/fi/d819tecu. The organization positions itself as an alternative to traditional banks, providing membership-based financial products tailored to transit worker needs and circumstances. Bill payment services are available both directly through their website and via third-party platforms like doxo.

As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, Division 819 operates on a cooperative model emphasizing member benefits and community focus within the transit employee demographic. The institution maintains a physical headquarters location and dedicated customer support phone line, indicating a commitment to direct member service. The credit union's services are primarily available to eligible members within the New Jersey service area, particularly those employed in transit-related occupations.

The main limitation of this credit union is its restricted membership eligibility—it serves primarily transit employees rather than the general public. The available information on third-party platforms like doxo is limited to auto loan bill payment options, suggesting a smaller service portfolio than major banks or broader credit unions. Members seeking comprehensive financial services including deposit accounts, mortgages, or investment services may need to verify availability directly, as the public-facing information focuses heavily on credit products. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Bill payment (online via website and doxo)
Online bill management through third-party platforms
Secure payment processing through doxo integration
pros: Member-owned cooperative structure with focus on transit employee community and needs
Established credit union with physical headquarters and dedicated customer support phone line (973-373-2332)
Offers auto loans and credit card products tailored to membership base
Multiple bill payment options including direct website payments and third-party integration (doxo)
Located in New Jersey with convenient Irvington headquarters address
Provides secure online bill payment through doxo with no fees when using linked bank account
Credit union model typically offers more favorable rates and terms than traditional banks
cons: Membership restricted to transit employees—not available to general public, limiting accessibility
Limited public information available; most details come from third-party payment processor rather than direct company sources
Only auto loans clearly documented in available sources; full product lineup not transparent on public directories
No apparent online banking platform or account opening information visible in search results
Limited service offerings compared to full-service credit unions or banks
best_for: Transit employees in New Jersey seeking member-owned financial institution with community focus
Public transportation workers needing auto financing with cooperative credit union advantages
Individuals eligible for Division 819 membership seeking credit products designed for transit worker circumstances
diagnosis: Best for transit employees in New Jersey who meet membership eligibility requirements and seek credit union-based financial services including auto loans and credit cards. The primary caveat is strict membership eligibility tied to transit employment, making this institution inaccessible to the general public; prospective members should verify employment qualification before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
